name: Valldemossa, Deià, and Coll de Sóller Loop
name_original: Montaña del Voltor — circular desde Valldemossa por Deià — Paraje Natural de la Sierra de Tramuntana
description: This difficult 26.5-mile mountain biking loop through Valldemossa, Deià, and Coll de Sóller offers stunning views and challenging terrain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Valldemossa, nestled within the Serra de Tramuntana mountains, offers diverse terrain for cyclists. The region is characterized by rugged mountain landscapes, deep valleys, and terraced hillsides adorned with ancient olive and almond trees. Valldemossa, at 412 meters above sea level, provides routes with steep ascents, thrilling descents, and winding bends, alongside a dramatic rocky coastline featuring secluded coves. Best mountain bike trails around Valldemossa The most popular mountain bike trail is Valldemossa, Deià, and Coll de Sóller Loop, a 26.5 miles (42.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 37 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers challenging terrain through mountain villages. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Deià – Deià Cove loop from Valldemossa, a difficult 32.0 miles (51.5 km) path. This route offers panoramic sea views and challenging terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Orange and Lemon Orchards – View of the Mediterranean Sea loop from Deià, a 11.3 miles (18.2 km) trail leading through picturesque orchards, often completed in about 1 hour 18 minutes. Mountain biking around Valldemossa is defined by rugged mountain terrain, deep valleys, and a rocky coastline. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult ascents and descents. The routes in Valldemossa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 6000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Valldemossa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Valldemossa?
A: There are over 200 mountain bike trails around Valldemossa. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 29 easy, 105 moderate, and 72 difficult options, ensuring a suitable ride for almost every mountain biker.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Valldemossa?
A: Mountain biking in Valldemossa, set within the Serra de Tramuntana, features rugged mountain terrain, deep valleys, and terraced hillsides. You'll encounter steep ascents, thrilling descents, and winding bends, often with panoramic views. Some trails also lead towards the dramatic rocky coastline and secluded coves.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Valldemossa?
A: Yes, many routes in Valldemossa are circular. For a challenging experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010373892/valldemossa-deia-and-coll-de-soller-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valldemossa, Deià, and Coll de Sóller Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 42.7 km trail. A moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40395214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orange and Lemon Orchards – View of the Mediterranean Sea loop from Deià&lt;/a&gt;, which is 18.2 km long.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Valldemossa?
A: The mountain bike trails around Valldemossa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 550 reviews. More than 6000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the challenging routes, stunning views, and diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly mountain bike trails in Valldemossa?
A: While the region is known for its challenging routes, there are 29 easy mountain bike trails available around Valldemossa. These provide a gentler introduction to the area's beautiful scenery for less experienced riders.

Q: What are some advanced mountain biking routes near Valldemossa?
A: For advanced riders, the region offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38256227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Deià – Deià Cove loop from Valldemossa&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 51.5 km path with panoramic sea views and challenging terrain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010373892/valldemossa-deia-and-coll-de-soller-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valldemossa, Deià, and Coll de Sóller Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 42.7 km trail through mountain villages.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails offer breathtaking views of the Serra de Tramuntana mountains and the coastline. You can find viewpoints like Mirador de Sa Foradada and Mirador de Miranda des Lledoners. Some routes also pass by significant natural features such as Puig des Teix, or lead to secluded coves like Port D'Es Canonge beach.

Q: Are there any cultural attractions or villages to explore near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, Valldemossa is surrounded by cultural highlights. You can explore charming villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/94862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fornalutx Village&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/79577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biniaraix&lt;/a&gt;. The town of Sóller is also nearby, featuring the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/94860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Constitution Square and Sant Bartomeu Church&lt;/a&gt;. Valldemossa itself boasts the Royal Carthusian Monastery and the Palace of King Sancho.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Valldemossa?
A: The Serra de Tramuntana, including Valldemossa, is popular for cycling year-round. However, spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November) offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the intense summer heat and potential winter chill at higher altitudes. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the Mediterranean Sea?
A: Absolutely. The region's dramatic rocky coastline ensures many trails provide stunning sea views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31445813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Deià – View of the Mediterranean Sea loop from Valldemossa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 34.4 km route specifically known for its coastal vistas.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that pass through orchards?
A: Yes, some routes wind through picturesque agricultural areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40395214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orange and Lemon Orchards – View of the Mediterranean Sea loop from Deià&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 18.2 km trail that takes you through beautiful orchards, offering a different scenic experience.

Q: Are there any caves or unique geological features to explore near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area around Valldemossa features several caves. You might find highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/275141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guillem Hermitage Cave&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/170080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de na Bernarda&lt;/a&gt;. These can offer interesting detours or points of interest during your ride.
tips: Wonderful rural town located in a valley in the center of the Sierra de Tramuntana. Its authentic stone alleys, where tranquility stands out, are absolutely beautiful. Undoubtedly one of the most charming towns on the island. The different viewpoints found in the town offer unbeatable panoramic views of the mountains.
Deia is a beautiful hillside town. The small food market is perfect for replenishing water and something sweet. Definitely plan it, because after that you need the grains.

If you can refrain from bathing in the sea, you should visit the picturesque bay in winter or spring. Out of season, it is absolutely lonely here and you can enjoy the view of the rocks and the blue sea just for you. Thanks to freshly laid out parking, it is not so lonely and picturesque in summer.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: This challenging 42.7 km mountain bike loop, with over 1,300 meters of elevation gain, typically takes around 4 hours and 37 minutes of riding time. However, given its difficulty and the stunning scenery, plan for a full day to allow for breaks, enjoying the views, and navigating technical sections.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this loop is rated as difficult and requires very good fitness and advanced mountain biking skills. You should be prepared for rocky and technical sections, steep ascents and descents, and potentially needing to dismount and push your bike on certain parts.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied and challenging. You'll encounter paved roads, especially on the ascent to Coll de Sóller, but also significant unpaved sections including natural paths, gravel, and technical singletrack. Expect rocky trails, steep climbs, and exhilarating descents, particularly around Valldemossa and Deià.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the Valldemossa, Deià, and Coll de Sóller Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop typically starts and ends in Valldemossa. You can find parking in or around the town. Valldemossa is well-connected by road, making it a convenient starting point for this circular route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The Serra de Tramuntana region is best enjoyed during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather is generally milder, and the landscapes are vibrant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential winter rains.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers numerous highlights. You'll pass through the charming village of Rosa Street, Valldemossa, and the picturesque town of Deià, perched on cliffs. You'll also get stunning coastal views, especially near Deià Cove, and panoramic mountain scenery from Coll de Sóller.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail in the Serra de Tramuntana?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride this mountain bike loop within the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park. However, always respect private property, stay on marked trails, and adhere to any local regulations or signage you may encounter.

faqs.question: Is the Valldemossa, Deià, and Coll de Sóller Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in the Serra de Tramuntana, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially given the technical nature of this mountain bike route and the presence of wildlife and livestock. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a long, difficult ride and that you carry enough water for both of you.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike loop connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several official trails. Significant portions of the loop follow sections of the GR 221 Dry Stone Route (Stage 3: Esporles - Can Boi and Stage 4: Can Boi - Muleta), as well as the Ruta de Pedra en Sec. You'll also find parts of [CIMA IB12] Coll de Sóller * Sóller and El Camí IT4TR4 El Camí Illenc: de l'Alguer a Formentera.

faqs.question: Are there any specific sections of the route that are particularly challenging or offer unique experiences?
faqs.answer: The ascent to Coll de Sóller, with its 60+ hairpin bends, is a well-known challenge. The old Ma-11A road to Coll de Sóller offers a quieter experience with low traffic since the tunnel opened. The 'Valldemossa climb' is also considered a 'hidden gem' with beautiful switchbacks and sea views, offering a more secluded ride compared to other popular routes.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the starting point of the loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, Valldemossa is accessible by public bus services from Palma. Check local bus schedules (e.g., TIB lines) for routes and timings to plan your journey to the starting point of the loop.
